macro_summary: Flow is thin and mixed. The dominant macro thread is continued de-escalation: oil extended its decline to four-month lows on expectations of smoother tanker flows through Hormuz, and Gulf equities eased alongside it (Business Recorder). The government separately confirmed the weekly oil-pricing mechanism stays in place -- OMCs get assurance of premium-based pricing to limit losses, though the same Dawn piece notes repeated revisions are discouraging foreign investment, i.e. partial reassurance after yesterday's Rs105bn OMC-loss warning, not a full resolution. Two real, named E&P operational positives landed: Mari Energies began Shams-1 gas supply to SNGPL, and OGDCL started gas production at the Sahito-1 discovery in Sindh. SIFC advanced the Machike-Tarru Jabba white-oil pipeline, a long-term logistics positive for the oil/refining chain. Separately, a Dawn 'Live Updates' page titled 'War returns to Iran with Israel, US strikes' appeared in today's PSO-tagged feed -- this is a live-blog headline, not corroborated by any other item in today's allowlisted flow (which otherwise points to de-escalation), so it is treated as ambiguous/low-confidence rather than a fresh escalation signal. PSX itself extended losses for a third straight session per Dawn's market wrap, despite the broader peace-process backdrop.

MARI.summary: Mari Energies commenced gas supply from its Shams-1 discovery to SNGPL -- a named, concrete production/revenue event corroborated across three allowlisted outlets.
headlines: Mari Energies begins gas supply from Shams-1 discovery to SNGPL - Profit by Pakistan Today

OGDC.summary: OGDCL started gas production at the Sahito-1 discovery well in Sindh, a fresh named operational positive, partially offset by oil prices extending their slide toward four-month lows.
headlines: Gas production at Sahito-1 begins - Business Recorder
OGDCL starts gas production from Sahito-1 discovery well in Sindh - Profit by Pakistan Today

PSO.summary: Government reaffirmed the weekly oil-pricing mechanism stays, assuring OMCs of premium-based pricing to limit losses -- a partial reassurance after yesterday's Rs105bn loss warning -- but the same piece flags investor-confidence costs from repeated revisions, and a separate ambiguous war-escalation live-blog headline (uncorroborated elsewhere today) adds noise rather than a clear directional signal.
headlines: Weekly oil pricing mechanism to stay - Dawn
SIFC clears way for strategic Machike-Tarru Jabba White Oil Pipeline - Business Recorder
Live Updates: War returns to Iran with Israel, US strikes - Dawn

PPL.summary: Gulf equities slipped as oil softened further on Iran-talks optimism, a near-term drag on E&P revenue assumptions, partially offset by the SIFC pipeline infrastructure advance.
headlines: Gulf equities slip as oil softens on Iran talks, Fed rate fears weigh on sentiment - Profit by Pakistan Today
